source: CT6-6 §9 (EVAL-1…17) + §12 (SEC) — the Evaluator / triage service on the v3.23.0 substrate; log->categorized issue, dedup tally, the quarantine rule across versions, and a signed-submission server

scenarios: logs become categorized, versioned, deduplicable issue records
acceptance_criteria: evaluator reviews logs as a senior agentic architect: categorize + root-cause
normalized issue record: what/what-happened + version (EVAL-8) + metadata (EVAL-9) + redacted evidence (EVAL-14) + dedup fingerprint
EVAL-3 ~hourly BG optimization task on bg_runtime

scenarios: duplicates collapse and recurring issues reach the backlog
acceptance_criteria: batch duplicates by fingerprint (count + representative + versions)
promote over-threshold entries to a longer-lasting backlog

scenarios: an old-version report with an intermediate similar fix is quarantined
acceptance_criteria: EVAL-11 quarantine: intermediate similar fix (seen < fix <= current) -> quarantine + verify_from
EVAL-12 first-occurrence: judged-already-fixed -> quarantine, else open
numeric version comparison (3.10 > 3.9); earliest intermediate fix chosen
EVAL-6 resolution log + may-already-be-fixed flag; EVAL-7/13 recurrence tracking; EVAL-5 two-stage core-issue review

scenarios: a signed submission is accepted; a forged/replayed one is rejected
acceptance_criteria: server verifies a signed Ed25519 envelope (reuse services/common/handshake.py); no per-user secret
privacy re-applied server-side (off stores nothing; summary allow-list structural + redacted evidence)
EVAL-2 GitHub-issue sink adapter (payload built; real POST injected)
services/triage stdlib-only core, design-not-deployed; live server/GitHub/Postgres/LLM/SEC-4 are adapters
full suite green cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1

testing_authorizations.playwright: N/A — no UI/frontend surface (a stdlib-only background service + tests).
testing_authorizations.dev_api_integration: N/A — the live triage HTTP server / GitHub API / Postgres / Anthropic LLM are external infra behind adapter boundaries (not in-repo); the deterministic core (evaluator parse, tally, quarantine, handshake-verify, privacy) is exercised by the pytest suite with FakeLLMClient + InMemorySink + in-process Ed25519 signing.

confirmed_stubs.why: the live socket + serve_forever is the operator's; the deterministic core is handle_submission, exercised without sockets — an honest boundary, not a gap

confirmed_stubs.stub: services/triage/sink.py GitHubIssueSink poster + the GitHub->server pull-back
confirmed_stubs.why: the real token + HTTP POST to the GitHub issues API, and pulling issues back, need the live API (the separated tier's own dep, REPO-4); the core builds the payload + injects the poster (InMemorySink for tests) — documented, not a gap

the project-unique genuine-logger proof is the separable closed/paid piece (SEC-4); the open core ships a pluggable verifier + the handshake's HMAC stub, and the docstring is explicit that anti-spam needs it enabled — by design, not a gap
the real server persists to Postgres (the separated tier's own dep, REPO-4); the in-memory TallyQueue is the testable deterministic core — documented, not a gap
